Title: Product
Header Title: We're building products that are transforming a $7 trillion industry.
Header Description: Product at hx sits at the intersection of deep technology, AI and a domain few companies are capable of tackling: specialty insurance pricing and underwriting.
Heading: Explore our Teams
Interactive / Body 1: Product Managers at hx work in one of the most technical product environments in fintech. We shape platform-level decisions, work deeply with engineering, and move between core product growth, new product creation, and new market expansion. Ownership evolves fast here - and PMs are expected to pair strong product instincts with genuine technical depth.
Interactive / Body 2: Product Operations at hx builds the systems that make an AI-native product organisation possible. This team designs the workflows, tooling, and automation that give PMs leverage - from AI-enabled context gathering to shared agent libraries. It's a forward-looking function focused on scale, speed, and coherence, not classic process policing.
Line 1: Operate without a playbook in a category we're still defining
Line 2: Navigate product constraints with full transparency
Line 3: Go deep on product choices that shape the entire stack
Line 4: Solve problems that only appear at scale and in production
Line 5: Spend more time in customer discovery calls than most PMs ever will
Section Subheading: Product is a craft-first team with a few key dimensions
Card Heading 1: The patterns of our very best product people
Block Title 1a: They care about product judgement, not just delivery
Block Text 1a: They are comfortable making calls without perfect information. They think in systems, trade-offs and second-order effects, and they take responsibility for the consequences of product decisions over time.
Block Title 1b: They go deep where it matters
Block Text 1b: They don't skim the surface. They are willing to stay in the technical detail, the workflow, or the customer context until the problem is genuinely understood - even when that takes longer than expected.
Block Text 1c: They shape direction before the brief is perfect or the roadmap is locked. They prioritise deliberately and adapt without losing coherence.
Block Title 2a: A platform where product judgement actually matters
Block Text 2a: Leveraging the real "human" skills of distilling insight, applying taste, and applying thoughtful judgement are critical and you're empowered to influence across a large product domain.
Block Text 2b: Engineering challenges assumptions. Domain experts push back. AI changes what is possible week to week. The work sharpens your thinking rather than narrowing it.
Block Text 2c: We develop technical fluency, platform thinking and AI literacy together. The learning curve is steep, but it is cumulative - not episodic.

Title: AI
Header Title: We build what AI-native insurance looks like.
Header Description: AI at hx combines applied research and product engineering in one of the most complex domains AI can tackle: insurance pricing and underwriting.
Heading: Explore our Teams
Interactive / Body 1: Research at hx is true R&D - exploration and experimentation, not just implementation. We explore the frontier of what language models can and can't do for actuarial and underwriting work, running experiments to uncover where AI creates transformational value. The work is rigorous, ambiguous, and high-stakes - and if we find something, it could reshape the industry.
Interactive / Body 2: Agents at hx takes proven concepts and scales them into production products. We own production AI products - improving accuracy, building trust, and shipping to customers who are already paying. The work blends product engineering with applied AI, and we're expected to move fast without breaking what's already working. This is 1-to-100 work, not science projects.
Line 1: We might be the only company in the world building a best-in-class actuarial agent.
Line 2: 0-to-1 on products no one else has shipped.
Line 3: Language models that reason, not just query.
Line 4: Force multipliers, not solo operators. Pro-code platform, vertical AI, bleeding-edge research.
Line 5: You'll learn in 3 months what takes a year elsewhere.
Section Heading: How the AI Team is structured
Section Subheading: AI is a specialist team with a few key dimensions
Card Heading 1: The patterns of our very best AI people
Block Title 1a: They're curious, not anxious, when the map is gray
Block Text 1a: They look at a completely unknown problem and get excited - they want to know what's behind the clouds. They thrive when the map is unclear and get energized by unknown problems.
Block Title 1b: They have grit - the rough kind
Block Text 1b: Not just persistence in an orderly environment. Grit like tunnelling through granite - sharp, uncomfortable, and unpredictable. They expect most things to fail, and they keep going when the problem complexity explodes beyond what they expected.
Block Title 1c: They leverage the team, not just deliver to it
Block Text 1c: They unlock what's in other people's heads - giving the right context, the right storytelling, to multiply creativity. Force multipliers, not solo operators.
Block Text 2a: We're building AI from first principles - applied research, production agents, infrastructure - and that depth stays with you.
Block Text 2b: No team leads yet. No engineering managers. Senior IC roles are open, management tracks are open. The team is scaling, it's strategic to the company, and it's working - which means continuous investment, not a 6-month experiment that gets cut.
Block Text 2c: a16z, Battery and Highland have invested in the biggest AI companies on the planet - and they expect hx to be another. AI isn't a side bet here. It's the mandate.

Title: Product Design
Header Description: Design at hx sits at the intersection of craft excellence, AI, and product complexity: where designers set the bar and are trusted with high-impact ownership from day one.
Heading: Explore our Teams
Interactive / Body 1: Product designers at hx own the parts of the product that customers live inside every day. We translate messy, often hidden specialty insurance workflows into clear interaction, usability, and visual design. We work end to end from discovery to detailed execution, using AI-native workflows and interactive prototypes, and partnering closely with front-end engineering. This pairing of craft excellence and deep domain learning means what we build feels human, reliable, and ready for real underwriting work.
Interactive / Body 2: Design engineers at hx shorten the distance between idea and working product. We take concepts closer to a real skeleton using AI-native tools, interactive prototypes and codebase exploration. This craft helps engineers pick work up with confidence, ensures designs stay feasible, and allows the team to explore further in the same amount of time without creating brittle one-offs.
Interactive / Body 3: Brand designers at hx shape how the market experiences us before anyone logs in. You build the external expression of hx - from website to campaigns - and keep it tightly aligned with what is happening inside the product. The role frames value others unlock, making highly technical insurance intelligence feel clear, confident and coherent at first impression.
Line 1: Most design teams add polish at the end - ours define what sells and ships.
Line 2: Autonomy is a feature of the job, not a perk you earn later.
Line 3: Design is a spectrum here, not a set of boxes.
Line 4: Partnership is the craft - without it the work doesn’t matter.
Line 5: Design decisions are made in conversation with engineering, not in isolation.
Line 6: We design enablement, not just features.
Section Subheading: Design is a craft-first team with a few key dimensions
Card Heading 1: The patterns of our very best design people
Block Title 1a: They lead with craft and conviction
Block Text 1a: They have a strong point of view on what excellence looks like. They move confidently across visual design, interaction and usability, and they care deeply about the details that make experiences work.
Block Title 1b: They embrace complexity, not surface fixes
Block Text 1b: They don't assume the domain is simple just because the tools look outdated. They develop real knowledge of insurance workflows, unwritten rules and customer context - and design experiences that respect that depth.
Block Text 1c: They treat feedback as a gift, not a threat. They can move a design left or right if it leads to a better outcome, and they help teammates do the same without ego getting in the way.
Block Title 2a: Design has a voice from day one
Block Text 2a: We're not decorating finished ideas. Design sits at the centre of how the product sells, works and feels. Our work materially affects business outcomes, and leadership expects us to have a point of view.
Block Text 2b: We own significant, meaningful work earlier than almost anywhere else. The team is small and flat. Influence comes from the quality of our thinking, not our title.
Block Text 2c: We work in a domain that is harder than it first appears - and we see the impact of our decisions in days and weeks, not quarters. The learning curve is steep, but the feedback loop is immediate.

Title: Sales & Partnerships
Header Title: 100% customer retention. 10x ROI. Now help us scale to a billion dollars ARR.
Header Description: Sales and Partnerships at hx sits at the intersection of enterprise selling, strategic relationships and industry transformation: where we work with the world's largest insurers.
Heading: Explore our Teams
Interactive / Body 1: Strategic Sales at hx owns the top 20-25 insurers in the world and is responsible for turning them into £10m ARR accounts. We're multi-threaded across 60+ champions, quarterbacking deals that can reach £40m TCV, and in direct conversation with Amrit and our investors weekly. This team delivers 70-75% of company revenue. There's no hiding - and no ceiling.
Interactive / Body 2: Enterprise Sales at hx opens new markets and lands new logos. We work across 15-20 accounts, entering territories like Saudi Arabia and new segments like MGAs. The work is more exploratory - finding product-market fit in new verticals and building the playbook as we go. Still big deals, still C-suite access, but with more greenfield opportunity.
Interactive / Body 3: Partnerships at hx builds the relationships that get us into rooms we wouldn't otherwise be in. We work with the advisory firms that brief the C-suite of our largest prospects daily, and the technology partners whose platforms sit at the core of their infrastructure. When a global insurer is building their AI strategy, we want to be in it - and partnerships makes that happen. It's revenue-driven, deeply cross-functional, and still being defined.
Line 1: Straight into the C-suite. CEO, CFO, Chief Actuary - not three layers down.
Line 2: Partnerships here isn't a support function - it's a revenue multiplier.
Line 3: We shape the product, not just sell it.
Line 4: When a global insurer picks a solution behind closed doors, we want allies in that room.
Line 5: High excellence, hugely collaborative. Rarely do you get both.
Line 6: We build relationships that last years, not quarters.

Title: Model Development
Header Title: We build the pricing intelligence that powers hx Renew.
Header Description: Model Development at hx sits at the intersection of production engineering, actuarial complexity and customer partnership: where we transform legacy pricing logic into modern, trusted models.
Heading: Explore our Teams
Interactive / Body 1: We're a collaborative, highly technical team that works directly with customers to build production-grade pricing and reinsurance models in hx Renew. We translate legacy Excel models or design new ones from scratch, support customers to own and extend their models, and work across global carriers, reinsurers and specialist MGAs. This is high-stakes, customer-facing work where we're often the main technical touchpoint customers have with hx. We need strong coding craft, clear communication under pressure, and the ability to move between technical depth and stakeholder conversations seamlessly.
Line 1: We explain complex logic to actuaries, IT teams and C-suite alike.
Line 2: Technical delivery with strategic consulting influence.
Line 3: Show and tell culture: solve it once, share it with everyone.
Line 4: Scaffolding at the start. Full ownership as we grow.
Line 5: We translate between actuarial thinking and production code.
Line 6: We work across Product, CS, Sales and Solutions Architecture daily.
Section Subheading: One team - focused on rapid, agile delivery
Card Heading 1: The patterns of our very best MDT people
Block Title 1a: They're comfortable in front of customers
Block Text 1a: Most of their time is on customer projects. They're often the main technical face customers see. They explain complex logic clearly and build trust with actuaries, IT teams and C-suite stakeholders under real pressure. They work directly with actuaries to understand pricing logic, business rules and edge cases, then rebuild it in a way that's maintainable, collaborative and performs at scale.
Block Title 1b: They develop production-grade code with care
Block Text 1b: Their code goes live with real underwriters supporting real business decisions. They develop from capable coder to building fully tested, maintainable models that hold up under scrutiny. This means attention to detail, clear documentation, and code that others can understand and extend.
Block Title 1c: They own outcomes, not just tasks
Block Text 1c: They stay involved beyond the initial build. They see projects through, support customers to extend their models, and take responsibility for whether the engagement succeeds or needs adjustment along the way. This means ongoing collaboration, not just handoff.
Block Text 2a: We start with strong support and senior partnership. As we grow, we lead engagements end to end and present at user groups. The team guides us to the edge of our comfort zone while owning the risk at leadership level.
Block Text 2b: Production-grade engineering and stakeholder collaboration under pressure. We learn how to write robust code and how to build trust with C-suite executives. Most people spend years building one or the other - we develop both simultaneously.
Block Text 2c: We move between global carriers, specialist MGAs and complex reinsurance structures. We see how different markets price risk, how different customers work, and we build relationships and models that matter.

Title: Actuarial Pricing & Value Consulting
Header Title: We help customers quantify pricing transformation.
Header Description: Actuarial Pricing & Value Consulting at hx sits at the intersection of actuarial expertise, commercial strategy and measurable impact: where we quantify transformation value and provide market credibility.

Interactive / Body 1: Pricing at hx is a pricing strategy and transformation role in high-stakes conversations with chief actuaries, CEOs and reinsurance leaders. Interactive / Body 2: Value Consulting quantifies what hx creates for customers and prospects - financially, operationally and strategically. We build pre-sale value cases, run post-sale assessments, turn market research into commercial insight and create frameworks that Sales, Customer Success and Marketing use daily. This is strategic, narrative-driven work that influences deals, accelerates velocity and reframes how customers see transformation.

Block Text 2a: We can have wild ideas and leadership will back us to try them. But if they don't work, we own the fix. Experimentation is expected, as is moving fast when things need course correction.
Block Title 2b: A team where expectations are explicit
Block Text 2b: We make priorities, progress, and decisions easy to see. That shared context enables trust, accountability, and strong performance, because everyone knows what matters and how to deliver it well.
Block Title 2c: Growth that moves at hx speed
Block Text 2c: We get real ownership early, and the space and support to test, learn, and iterate fast. The pace and responsibility mean we level up quickly, and our experience compounds in a way that is unmatchable elsewhere.

Title: Talent
Header Title: Designing the hiring engine behind a generational business.
Header Description: We're building a company designed to last for decades - and that means every hire matters. Talent at hx exists to protect and elevate the hiring bar, so the people who join strengthen the company and grow with it: stretched by the work, supported in the craft, and proud of the careers they build here.
Heading: Explore our Teams
Interactive / Body 1: We're genuinely excited about how AI helps us do more of what we love - connecting deeply with candidates and helping them see why hx could be the place they truly thrive. Talent at hx is all in on AI as leverage. We measure our wins across the full spectrum - quality, pace, and the time we've reclaimed to invest back into what matters most to the craft: judgement, connection, and thoughtful decision-making.
Interactive / Body 2: We sit inside the business and are trusted with real influence. We've earned that through consistent hard work, high standards, and showing up when decisions matter. Our Talent Partners work closely with leaders to shape teams in practice: rescoping roles, challenging assumptions, uplevelling expectations, and influencing how work is structured and who fills it. Hiring here is a genuinely shared responsibility, and that level of voice and trust is rare at our stage.
Line 1: Hold conviction when “good enough” tries to pass
Line 2: Coach leaders to interview with discipline
Line 3: Measure impact after the offer and let it change how you hire
Line 4: Treat hiring as company-building, not coordination
Line 5: Design AI-native workflows that amplify human judgement
Line 6: Run a process you’d be proud to go through yourself
Section Subheading: Talent at the edge of their craft
Card Heading 2: To thrive in Talent at hx, you will need
Block Text 1a: We protect the hiring bar because we understand what dilution does over time. We're willing to pass on good candidates to hold out for great ones. We look beyond skills to judgment, drive and cultural add. Talent density is where exceptional people choose to stay.
Block Title 1b: We think in systems and move fast inside them
Block Text 1b: We design hiring as infrastructure. We surface trade-offs, use data to make decisions visible, and remove manual drag wherever we can. We experiment. We own outcomes. If something can be better, we change it.
Block Text 1c: Autonomy is real here. We're trusted to test ideas, redesign workflows, challenge assumptions and shape real outcomes - and we're accountable for the results.
Block Text 2a: Hiring defines who builds hx and therefore what gets built. We're not filling roles - we're shaping the trajectory of the company. Every decision ripples through teams, products and outcomes. That weight is real. So is the responsibility that comes with it.
Block Text 2b: We work directly with senior leaders as strategic partners. We challenge thinking, design hiring strategy together, and refine the bar as the business evolves. Our perspective carries weight. We're expected to push back when it matters.
Block Text 2c: We work hard. We care deeply about craft. We meme daily and build inside language that makes no sense to outsiders. Standards are high. Energy is real. If you need everything to be serious and corporate, this won't feel right.

Title: Finance
Header Description: Finance at hx builds the engine that lets the company move quickly and experiment boldly while staying in control. We turn complex, high-growth reality into something leaders can actually steer.
Heading: Explore our Teams
Interactive / Body 1: FP&A at hx is where finance, data and storytelling meet. We build models and scenarios that show what the future could look like, then help leaders understand what to do about it. We work across systems, automate reporting and spend more time on insight than spreadsheet gymnastics.
Interactive / Body 2: Financial Control at hx designs and runs the financial infrastructure of the company. We own the close, controls and core processes, and we're always looking for ways to improve them. We care about accurate data, clean systems and automation that removes repetitive work for everyone.
Line 1: We're in the room early, shaping decisions.
Line 2: We're building processes, not Excel temples.
Line 3: Impact means changing behavior, not just sharing data.
Line 4: AI native by design, not by experiment.
Line 5: Automate yourself out of a job, then move up the value chain.
Line 6: We're synthesizing and filtering data, not just creating tables.
Section Subheading: Finance is a lean, AI-native team with two specialist areas
Card Heading 1: The patterns of our very best finance people
Block Title 1a: They connect the dots across systems
Block Text 1a: They're constantly marrying systems together - understanding how one piece of data in Ashby expands across finance, HR and forecasting. They follow threads until they find good data or missing data, never losing sight of what the whole picture needs to look like.
Block Title 1b: They build processes, then let them run
Block Text 1b: They build workflows that can tell them when there's a problem, then they let them go. The unlock in the age of AI is setting things up so they run without constant attention - then moving to higher-value work.
Block Title 1c: They focus on intellectual curiosity and love of learning
Block Text 1c: The world is changing faster than anyone can predict. They're confident that if they want to understand something, they can.
Block Title 2a: Freedom to automate yourself out of a job
Block Text 2a: We're given enough of a blank sheet that we define the problem and why it matters - not how to solve it. We can run off in a different direction if we're confident there's benefit there. Even if 9 out of 10 experiments fail to work, the 1 that does will 100x the rest.
Block Title 2b: Access to decisions that matter, early
Block Text 2b: We get exposure to leadership decisions, board-level work and strategic trade-offs far earlier than most finance careers. We're in the room when ideas are forming, not signing off after everything's agreed.
Block Title 2c: Compressed learning at the cutting edge
Block Text 2c: One year here feels like several elsewhere. We're building AI-native finance in real time - learning systems thinking, data fluency and commercial judgement that will age well.

Title: Legal & InfoSec
Header Description: Legal, infosec and business ops at hx sit at the centre of the company: helping hx ship ambitious products, close demanding customers and adopt AI quickly while staying secure and compliant.
Heading: Explore our Teams
Interactive / Body 1: Legal at hx sits inside the flow of the business - navigating complex enterprise deals, shaping AI and privacy strategy, guiding procurement and supporting product decisions. We work in the legal function and help build it.
Interactive / Body 2: InfoSec at hx has a clear job: protect hx, protect our customers, keep us off the front page. We design and maintain the security posture that lets us sell into highly regulated organisations. We're part enabler, part guardian - making sure the business can work quickly without opening doors it doesn't mean to.
Interactive / Body 3: Business ops & automation at hx is less about running a central ops team and more about seeding operational thinking across the company. You think in systems - how data, tools and teams connect. You help design workflows that use automation and AI instead of extra headcount.
Line 1: We're the calm when there's chaos.
Line 2: Protect hx and keep us off the front page.
Line 3: No old playbook to inherit - you build the function.
Line 4: AI native legal and security, not bolt-ons.
Line 5: Pragmatic over dogmatic. Safe and shippable, not just compliant.
Line 6: We protect what we've built and enable what comes next.
Section Subheading: Product is a craft-first team with a few key dimensions
Card Heading 1: The patterns of our very best Legal & InfoSec people
Block Text 1a: They know "secure and compliant" has to coexist with "shippable and usable". They find ways to say "yes, and here's how" rather than hiding behind policy or defaulting to no.
Block Title 1b: They build systems that outlast them
Block Text 1b: They design workflows, frameworks and tools that the business can run without constant input - then move on to the next problem. They create infrastructure, not dependency.
Block Title 1c: They stay calm when stakes are high
Block Text 1c: They keep their composure. When there's an incident, a deal on the line, or something's gone wrong, they're the steady voice that says "okay, let's work through this".
Block Text 2a: We're not asked to enforce rules from a handbook. We're trusted to make judgement calls, balance trade-offs, and find solutions that actually work in the real world.
Block Text 2b: We work directly with execs, customers and cross-functional leaders from early on. We see decisions being made, not just the slides afterwards.
Block Title 2c: A legacy you can point to
Block Text 2c: We help build Legal and InfoSec at hx. The frameworks, systems and practices we create become part of how hx operates for years to come.
